titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir John Robert O'Connell to Sir Matthew Nathan, 27 March 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Robert O'Connell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Sir John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943), a solicitor, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). O'Connell writes to apply for the vacant position of chief crown solicitor for Ireland following the death of it's previous holder, Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916). O'Connell mentions that he has practised law in Dublin for over twenty-five years and that he is a member of the Senate of the University of Dublin. O'Connell had also acted as solicitor to Sir Jonathan Pim during his time as attorney general for Ireland. He continues to mention the various experiences he has attained through his legal work and hopes that the public experience he has gained will be taken into consideration.
Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/10

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Telegrams Lawyers Dublin
Telephone No. 1462 34, Kildare Street,
Dublin. 27th March 1916. Dear Sir Matthew Nathan, Owing to the death
of my friend the late Sir
Malachy Kelly a vacancy
occurs in the office of Chief
Crown Solicitor for Ireland
and I venture to ask
you to bear in mind
my claims in any
influence you may exercise
in the appointment to that
office. I have now been
for over twenty five years
in very extensive practice
in the City of Dublin. I
am a Graduate both in
Arts and in Law and a
Member of the Senate of the
University of Dublin. I
have had a great deal
of official experience as
the Chairman of the Loan
Fund Board for many
years until its dissolution
and, I acted as Solicitor
for the Rt. Honourable Jonathan
Pine during the period
of his Attorney Generalship
I have written a good
deal on the Housing
Problem in Ireland and
on the various subjects
such as Infant Mortality
Street trading of children
re connected with it and
have given practical service
to this cause as a Director
of the Association for the
Housing of the very poor
and I think I may claim
that it was I who rendered
the holding of the Civics
Exhibition — subsequently
closed as the result of the
Hall barricade being
required for Military purposes
at the outbreak of the War —
in 1914, possible. Pray forgive these
somewhat personal
details but I venture
to hope that the public
service, I have been so
fortunate as to be able to
render are an element
which ought to be taken
into consideration Believe me
Yours Sincerely John Robert O' Connell The Rt. Honble Sir Matthew Nathan
G.C.M.G.
Under Secretary Lodge
Phoenix Park Dublin
